fkcompute.domain — Braid and Constraint Domain¶

The domain subpackage contains all pure mathematical logic with no I/O or side effects. It is organised into two main areas:

  • Braid — data structures for braid words, topological data, and sign assignments.

  • Constraints — classes and reduction algorithms for the constraint system derived from R-matrix equations.

fkcompute.domain.braid¶

Braid Topology¶

class fkcompute.domain.braid.topology.BraidTopology(braid: List[int])[source]¶

Bases: object

Pure topological data for a braid closure.

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Attributes set by __init__

Attribute

Description

braid

The braid word as list[int].

max_strand

Largest generator index present.

n_strands

Total number of strands (max_strand + 1).

n_crossings

Number of crossings (len(braid)).

crossing_signs

list[int] of +1 / −1 per crossing.

writhe

Sum of crossing signs.

n_components

Number of components in the braid closure.

states

Canonical state representatives (strand, column) tuples.

state_equivalence_classes

Groups of locations that map to the same state.

strand_endpoints

Dict mapping component index → list of endpoint location pairs.
get_component(location: Tuple[int, int]) → int | None[source]¶

Get the component index for a given location.

get_state(location: Tuple[int, int]) → Tuple[int, int] | None[source]¶

Get the state at a given location.

label_from_location(location) → str[source]¶

Get the label for a location.

a_resolution_components() → Tuple[int, List][source]¶

Compute the A-resolution components.

b_resolution_components() → Tuple[int, List][source]¶

Compute the B-resolution components.

Lobb(L: int) → bool[source]¶

Check the Lobb bound.

DL(L: int) → bool[source]¶

Check the DL bound.

Signed Braid¶

class fkcompute.domain.braid.signed.SignedBraid(topology: BraidTopology)[source]¶

Bases: object

A braid with sign assignment data.

Holds a BraidTopology reference and adds sign-dependent state: strand_signs, matrices, r_matrices, sign_assignment.

Parameters:

topology – BraidTopology instance providing the pure topological data.

compute_r_matrices() → int | None[source]¶

Compute R-matrices for each crossing based on sign assignments.

validate() → bool[source]¶

Validate the current sign assignment.

none_count(matrix: List[List[Any]]) → int[source]¶

Count the number of None values in a matrix.

sign_at_position(position: Tuple[int, int]) → int | None[source]¶

Get the sign at a given position in the crossing matrices.

generate_strand_signs()[source]¶

Generate strand signs from the current assignment.

generate_position_assignments()[source]¶

Generate position assignments from strand signs.

compute_matrices()[source]¶

Compute crossing matrices from strand signs.

update(position: Tuple[int, int], value: int)[source]¶

Update a position in the crossing matrices.

propagate()[source]¶

Propagate sign constraints through the crossing matrices.

get_state_relations() → List[source]¶

Generate state relations for the current braid and sign assignment.

Combines four types of constraints: 1. Boundary conditions — Zero/NegOne for the first strand 2. Periodicity — Alias wrapping states around closure 3. Sign bounds — Leq bounds based on sign assignment 4. Crossing constraints — R-matrix inequalities + Conservation

reduced_relations() → List[source]¶

Get the reduced relations for this braid.

free_variables() → List[source]¶

Get the free variables after reduction.

load_sign_data(sign_data: List[int])[source]¶

Load sign data into the braid states.

Braid States (High-Level Wrapper)¶

class fkcompute.domain.braid.states.BraidStates(braid: List[int])[source]¶

Bases: SignedBraid

Represents a braid and its associated state information.

This is a backward-compatible wrapper that creates a BraidTopology and initializes a SignedBraid from it.

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

BraidStates is the primary entry point for braid computations. It combines BraidTopology and SignedBraid and provides the get_state_relations() method used by the pipeline.

Example:

from fkcompute.domain.braid.states import BraidStates

bs = BraidStates([1, 1, 1])
print(bs.n_components)  # 1
print(bs.writhe)        # 3

relations = bs.get_state_relations()
print(f"{len(relations)} constraints generated")

Braid Word Utilities¶

Braid word operations.

This module contains functions for working with braids (braid closures). A braid is a list of integers, where each integer represents a crossing.

CONVENTION: strand 0 is the top strand, strand 1 is the second strand, etc. CONVENTION: generators are indexed starting from 1, to number of strands - 1 Positive integers n represents strand n-1 crossing over strand n.

fkcompute.domain.braid.word.is_positive_braid(braid: List[int]) → bool[source]¶

Check if a braid is positive (all crossings have positive sign).

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

True if all generators are positive, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

fkcompute.domain.braid.word.is_homogeneous_braid(braid: List[int]) → bool[source]¶

Check if a braid is homogeneous.

A braid is homogeneous if for each generator index, either only positive or only negative crossings appear (but not both).

Parameters:

braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

True if the braid is homogeneous, False otherwise.

Return type:

bool

fkcompute.domain.braid.word.strand_path(strand: int, braid: List[int]) → List[List[Tuple[int, int]]][source]¶

Compute the path of a strand through a braid.

Parameters:

  • strand – The starting strand index (0-indexed).

  • braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

Returns:

List of path segments, where each segment is a list of (x, y) coordinates.

Return type:

list

fkcompute.domain.braid.word.braid_svg(braid: List[int], state_annotations=None, crossing_annotations=None) → str[source]¶

Generate an SVG representation of a braid.

Parameters:

  • braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

  • state_annotations – Optional list of (i, j, text) tuples for state annotations.

  • crossing_annotations – Optional list of (i, j, text) tuples for crossing annotations.

Returns:

SVG string representation of the braid.

Return type:

str

fkcompute.domain.braid.word.draw_braid(braid: List[int], state_annotations=None, crossing_annotations=None)[source]¶

Display a braid visualization in a Jupyter notebook.

Parameters:

  • braid – Braid word as a list of signed generator indices.

  • state_annotations – Optional list of (i, j, text) tuples for state annotations.

  • crossing_annotations – Optional list of (i, j, text) tuples for crossing annotations.

State Types¶

State type definitions for braid computations.

This module defines the StateLiteral class and special state constants used throughout the FK computation.

class fkcompute.domain.braid.types.StateLiteral(state: int)[source]¶

Bases: object

A literal state value in the braid state system.

Represents fixed states like zero (0) and negative unity (-1) that are used as boundary conditions in the constraint system.

fkcompute.domain.constraints¶

Constraint Relation Classes¶

Constraint relation classes for FK computation.

This module defines the constraint types used in the FK constraint system: - Leq: Less than or equal inequality - Less: Strict less than inequality - Zero: Equality to zero state - NegOne: Equality to negative one state - Alias: Equality between two states - Conservation: Sum conservation constraint

The constraint classes form a hierarchy:

Constraint (ABC)
├── Leq          — first ≤ second
├── Less         — first < second
├── Zero         — state = [0]
├── NegOne       — state = [-1]
├── Alias        — state ≡ alias
└── Conservation — Σ inputs = Σ outputs

Leq and Less encode ordering between states.

Zero and NegOne pin a state to the special values ZERO_STATE and NEG_ONE_STATE.

Alias identifies two state variables as equal; the constraint reducer uses this to eliminate variables.

Conservation encodes the R-matrix conservation law: the sum of incoming state values equals the sum of outgoing values at each crossing.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Constraint[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Base class for all constraint relations in the FK system.

abstract variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Leq(first: Any, second: Any)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Less than or equal constraint: first <= second.

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Less(first: Any, second: Any)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Strict less than constraint: first < second.

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Zero(state: Any)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Zero state constraint: state = [0].

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.NegOne(state: Any)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Negative one state constraint: state = [-1].

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Alias(state: Any, alias: Any)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Alias constraint: state and alias are equal.

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.relations.Conservation(inputs: List, outputs: List)[source]¶

Bases: Constraint

Conservation constraint: sum of inputs = sum of outputs.

variables() → List[source]¶

Return the list of variables involved in this constraint.

try_sum_alias() → Tuple[Any, List] | None[source]¶

Try to extract a sum alias from this conservation constraint.

Returns None if both inputs and outputs have more than one element. Otherwise returns (alias, sum_vars) where alias = sum(sum_vars).

Constraint Reduction¶

Constraint reduction and propagation for FK computation.

This module provides functions for reducing and simplifying constraint systems through various propagation rules.

full_reduce runs a fixed-point loop applying:

  1. Alias propagation — substitutes alias pairs throughout all other constraints.

  2. Constant propagation — resolves Zero/NegOne constraints by substituting their values everywhere.

  3. Variable elimination — removes constraints that become trivially satisfied after substitution.

The loop terminates when no more reductions can be applied.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.get_zeros(relations: List) → List[source]¶

Get all states constrained to be zero.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.get_neg_ones(relations: List) → List[source]¶

Get all states constrained to be negative unity.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.get_aliases(relations: List) → Dict[source]¶

Get all alias relations as a dictionary.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.get_sum_aliases(relations: List) → List[source]¶

Get all sum aliases from conservation relations.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.propagate_zero_aliases(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Propagate zero constraints through aliases.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.propagate_neg_one_aliases(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Propagate negative one constraints through aliases.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.de_alias_inequalities(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Replace aliased variables in inequalities with their canonical forms.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.symmetric_inequality(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Detect symmetric inequalities that imply equality.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.conservation_zeros(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Remove zeros from conservation constraints.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.conservation_alias(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Replace aliased variables in conservation constraints.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.unary_conservation_is_alias(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Convert unary conservation constraints to aliases.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.is_vacuous(relation: Any) → bool[source]¶

Check if a relation is vacuously true.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.remove_vacuous(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Remove vacuously true relations.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.reduce_relations(relations: List, verbose: bool = False) → List[source]¶

Apply all reduction rules once.

Rule order matters: de-aliasing inequalities first allows symmetric_inequality to detect equalities. Propagation rules then simplify through the new equalities. Conservation rules run after propagation to benefit from resolved aliases/zeros. Vacuous removal runs last to clean up any trivially true results.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.full_reduce(relations: List, verbose: bool = False, max_depth: int = 20) → List[source]¶

Fully reduce relations by repeatedly applying reduction rules until fixed point.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.all_variables(relations: List) → List[source]¶

Get all variables appearing in relations.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.reduction.free_variables(relations: List) → List[source]¶

Get all free (unbound) variables in relations.

Symbolic Constraint System¶

Symbol class for linear algebra in FK computation.

This module provides a Symbol class that represents linear expressions with integer coefficients, used for symbolic manipulation of constraints.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.symbols.symbols(n: int) → List[Symbol][source]¶

Create n symbols indexed from 1 to n.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.symbols.Symbol(index: int = 0)[source]¶

Bases: object

A symbolic linear expression with integer coefficients.

Represents expressions like: a + 2*b - 3*c + 5 where a, b, c are variables indexed by position in the internal array.

The internal representation is a numpy array where: - var[0] is the constant term - var[i] for i > 0 is the coefficient of variable i

__init__(index: int = 0)[source]¶

Create a symbol. index=0 creates a constant 1, index>0 creates variable x_index.

subs(dictionary: Dict[Symbol, Symbol | int]) → Symbol[source]¶

Substitute values for symbols.

as_coefficients_dict() → Dict[Symbol, float][source]¶

Return dictionary mapping symbols to their coefficients.

constant() → float[source]¶

Return the constant term.

is_constant() → bool[source]¶

Check if this symbol is a constant (no variables).

free_symbols() → List[Symbol][source]¶

Return list of symbols with non-zero coefficients.

index(a: Symbol | None = None) → int[source]¶

Get the index of this symbol (if elementary) or of symbol a.

An elementary symbol has exactly one non-zero coefficient (which is 1).

fkcompute.domain.constraints.symbols.solve(symbol: Symbol, index: int | Symbol) → List[Symbol][source]¶

Solve for a variable in a symbolic expression.

Given symbol = 0, solve for the variable at index. Returns a list containing the solution.

ConstraintSystem dataclass for FK computation.

Represents the fully processed constraint system ready for ILP generation.

class fkcompute.domain.constraints.system.ConstraintSystem(assignment: ~typing.Dict, degree_criteria: ~typing.Dict, multi_var_inequalities: ~typing.List[~fkcompute.domain.constraints.symbols.Symbol], single_var_signs: ~typing.Dict, raw_relations: ~typing.List = <factory>, reduced_relations: ~typing.List = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

The fully processed constraint system, ready for ILP generation.

assignment

Mapping from state locations to their symbolic expressions.

Type:

Dict

degree_criteria

Mapping from component index to its degree constraint expression.

Type:

Dict

multi_var_inequalities

List of symbolic expressions where 0 <= expr must hold.

Type:

List[fkcompute.domain.constraints.symbols.Symbol]

single_var_signs

Mapping from elementary symbols to their signs (+1.0 or -1.0).

Type:

Dict

raw_relations

Original relations before reduction (for debugging).

Type:

List

reduced_relations

Relations after fixed-point reduction (for debugging).

Type:

List

assignment: Dict
degree_criteria: Dict
multi_var_inequalities: List[Symbol]
single_var_signs: Dict
raw_relations: List
reduced_relations: List

Phase 2 pipeline entry point for FK computation.

Provides a single clear function that performs the full Phase 2 flow: raw constraints -> reduction -> symbolic assignment -> criteria extraction.

fkcompute.domain.constraints.pipeline.build_constraint_system(braid_states, raw_relations: List = None) → ConstraintSystem[source]¶

Phase 2: Build and reduce the constraint system for a signed braid.

Steps: 1. Generate raw constraints (boundary, periodicity, sign bounds, crossing relations) 2. Reduce constraints to minimal form (fixed-point simplification) 3. Assign symbolic variables to free states 4. Extract degree criteria and inequalities

Parameters:

  • braid_states – A BraidStates (or SignedBraid) object with sign data loaded.

  • raw_relations – Pre-computed raw relations. If None, generated from braid_states.

Returns:

The fully processed constraint system ready for ILP generation.

Return type:

ConstraintSystem

fkcompute.domain.solver¶

Variable assignment functions for FK computation.

This module provides functions for computing symbolic variable assignments from constraint systems.

fkcompute.domain.solver.assignment.equivalence_assignment(assignment: Dict, braid_states) → Dict[source]¶

Extend assignment to all equivalent state locations.

Parameters:

  • assignment – Current variable assignment dictionary.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object containing state equivalence classes.

Returns:

Extended assignment including all equivalent states.

Return type:

dict

fkcompute.domain.solver.assignment.find_expressions(relations: List, assignment: Dict, braid_states) → List[source]¶

Find conservation expressions that can be used to solve for variables.

Parameters:

  • relations – List of constraint relations.

  • assignment – Current variable assignment.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

Returns:

List of symbolic expressions from conservation constraints.

Return type:

list

fkcompute.domain.solver.assignment.minimal_free(expressions: List, new: Dict = None, verbose: bool = False) → Dict[source]¶

Find minimal set of free variables by eliminating through expressions.

Parameters:

  • expressions – List of symbolic expressions to use for elimination.

  • new – Dictionary of substitutions found so far.

  • verbose – Whether to print progress.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping symbols to their expressions in terms of remaining free variables.

Return type:

dict

fkcompute.domain.solver.assignment.extend_variable_assignment(reduced_relations: List, partial_assignment: Dict, braid_states, verbose: bool = False) → Dict[source]¶

Extend a partial variable assignment to all variables.

Uses reduced relations to propagate assignments from assigned variables to unassigned ones through zeros, nunities, aliases, and sum aliases.

Parameters:

  • reduced_relations – List of reduced constraint relations.

  • partial_assignment – Initial partial assignment of variables to symbols.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

  • verbose – Whether to print progress.

Returns:

Complete assignment of all variables.

Return type:

dict

fkcompute.domain.solver.assignment.symbolic_variable_assignment(relations: List, braid_states) → Dict[source]¶

Create a symbolic variable assignment for a constraint system.

Assigns fresh symbols to free variables, then extends the assignment to all variables using the constraint relations.

Parameters:

  • relations – List of reduced constraint relations.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

Returns:

Complete symbolic assignment of all state variables.

Return type:

dict

Shared symbolic constraint processing for FK computation.

This module contains functions used by both Phase 1 (inversion/validation) and Phase 2 (solver/ilp) for processing symbolic constraints: - _minimum_degree_symbolic: compute degree constraints from crossing data - _inequality_manager: extract single/multiple variable inequalities - _process_assignment: combine the above into criteria, multiples, singlesigns

fkcompute.domain.solver.symbolic_constraints.minimum_degree_symbolic(assignment: Dict, braid_states, verbose: bool = False) → Dict[source]¶

Compute minimum degree constraints symbolically.

Parameters:

  • assignment – Symbolic variable assignment.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

  • verbose – Whether to print progress.

Returns:

Dictionary mapping components to their degree constraints.

Return type:

dict

fkcompute.domain.solver.symbolic_constraints.inequality_manager(relations: List, assignment: Dict, braid_states)[source]¶

Process inequalities from relations into single and multiple variable forms.

Parameters:

  • relations – List of constraint relations.

  • assignment – Variable assignment dictionary.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

Returns:

(singles, multiples) lists of inequality expressions.

Return type:

tuple

fkcompute.domain.solver.symbolic_constraints.process_assignment(assignment: Dict, braid_states, relations: List)[source]¶

Process an assignment to extract criteria, multi-variable inequalities, and single-variable signs.

Parameters:

  • assignment – Variable assignment dictionary.

  • braid_states – BraidStates object.

  • relations – List of constraint relations.

Returns:

(criteria, multi_var_inequalities, single_var_signs)

Return type:

tuple
